prose:
Fukui reactivity indices were computed for acrolein (8 atoms: O, C, C, C, H, H, H, H) using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level of theory (tier 'standard') in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. The N±1 states used charge +1/multiplicity 2 (cation) and charge −1/multiplicity 2 (anion). Partial charges were obtained via the Mulliken scheme. The geometry was taken as-is from the user-supplied acrolein.xyz (no re-optimization at DFT level). All three SCFs converged; integrity checks passed (sum(f+) = 1.0000, sum(f-) = 1.0000; trustworthy: true).

Headline result (most electrophilic site): β-carbon C4 (atom index 3, zero-based), f⁺ = 0.64232102.
Most nucleophilic site: carbonyl oxygen O1 (atom index 0, zero-based), f⁻ = 0.39388616.

Full per-atom Fukui indices (atoms: O, C, C, C, H, H, H, H):
  f⁺:  [0.40136419, 0.022255105, 0.43404673, 0.64232102, -0.1572595, -0.2072029, -0.062116996, -0.073407645]
  f⁻:  [0.39388616, 0.0059884353, 0.091403097, 0.087369815, 0.085961423, 0.1856796, 0.084485809, 0.065225658]
  f⁰:  [0.39762517, 0.01412177, 0.26272491, 0.36484542, -0.03564904, -0.010761648, 0.011184406, -0.004090993]
  dual descriptor: [0.0074780351, 0.01626667, 0.34264363, 0.5549512, -0.24322092, -0.39288251, -0.14660281, -0.1386333]

These results correctly predict Michael (1,4) addition at the β-carbon and electrophilic attack at the oxygen, consistent with acrolein's known α,β-unsaturated carbonyl reactivity.
